articles.title: Xinemarte Set to Transform Asian Cinemas Into Experiential Venues
articles.description: Creative technology company SQNXR is bringing its Xinemarte cinematic art platform to Asia.
articles.content:
Creative technology company SQNXR is bringing its Xinemarte cinematic art platform to Asia, transforming underutilized cinema spaces into premium immersive audiovisual experiences.
The rollout is scheduled to begin in May and the rest of the year in ... [1809 chars]

articles.title: Key Ministries may soon get the power to block online content
articles.description: The government is considering expanding the remit of the Information Technology Act, 2000 to empower critical ministries such as defence, home affairs, external affairs and information and broadcasting to issue content blocking orders under Section 69(A) of the legislation, officials told ET.
articles.content:

articles.title: Memory chipmaker Micron's revenue beats estimates on demand surge from AI boom
articles.description: March 18 (Reuters) - Chipmaker ⁠Micron Technology forecast third-quarter revenue above Wall Street ⁠expectations on Wednesday, betting on a surge in demand ‌for its memory chips used in artificial intelligence hardware.

articles.title: AI Ethics and the Future of Filmmaking Debated at FilMart Forum
articles.description: AI Ethics and the Future of Filmmaking Debated at FilMart Forum: ‘If We Cannot Stop Technology, Then We Should Be Asking What We Can Gain From It.”
articles.content:
AI and its rapidly expanding role in film and television production took center stage at a FilMart, which gathered five industry veterans across technology, directing and production to discuss the hot topic.

articles.title: HSBC Mulls Deep Job Cuts From Multiyear AI-Fueled Overhaul
articles.description: HSBC Holdings Plc is weighing deep job cuts over the coming years as Chief Executive Officer Georges Elhedery bets on AI to shrink its middle and back offices, one of the first signs of how the technology could reshape Wall Street workforces.
articles.content:
HSBC Holdings Plc is weighing deep job cuts over the coming years as Chief Executive Officer Georges Elhedery bets on AI to shrink its middle and back offices, one of the first signs of how the technology could reshape Wall Street workforces.
